daily_analyst_summary: Today’s intelligence highlights a surge in remote code execution and command injection vulnerabilities primarily driven by improper input validation across web interfaces, notably impacting Ui Unifi OS Server, Ivanti Standalone Sentry, and Splunk. Exploitation trends emphasize attackers leveraging path traversal and crafted HTTP requests to gain unauthorized access, with critical weaknesses also identified in Oracle Peoplesoft, Cisco Unified Communications Manager, and Lantronix firmware. Organizations should prioritize patching these high-risk CVEs to mitigate potential widespread compromise.
daily_ciso_summary: The current vulnerability landscape presents significant business risks, with multiple critical flaws across widely used enterprise platforms enabling remote code execution and unauthorized access, thereby exposing ACME Services to potential data breaches and operational disruptions. These vulnerabilities, particularly in key financial and communication systems like Oracle Peoplesoft, Cisco Unified Communications, and Splunk, heighten the risk of financial loss through data theft, fraud, or service downtime. We recommend prioritizing patch management, strengthening input validation controls, and enhancing monitoring on web interfaces and file upload functionalities to mitigate exploitation risks effectively.

cves.summary_analyst: CVE-2026-10520 affects Ivanti Standalone Sentry by allowing unauthorized attackers to execute arbitrary code due to improper validation of user input in the web interface.
cves.summary_ciso: CVE-2026-10520 poses a significant risk to our organization by potentially exposing sensitive data through Ivanti Standalone Sentry, requiring immediate action to mitigate the threat and protect our assets.
cves.description: CVE-2026-10520 affects Ivanti Standalone Sentry by allowing unauthorized attackers to execute arbitrary code due to improper validation of user input in the web interface.

news_article.title: Ivanti Sentry pre-auth RCE (CVE-2026-10520) – CVSS 10.0, public PoC, CISA KEV

cves.summary_analyst: CVE-2026-35273 is a vulnerability in Oracle Peoplesoft Enterprise Peopletools that allows an attacker to exploit a path traversal issue in the file upload functionality, potentially enabling unauthorized access to sensitive files on the server.
cves.summary_ciso: CVE-2026-35273 poses a significant risk to Oracle Peoplesoft Enterprise Peopletools, potentially exposing sensitive organizational data to unauthorized access, necessitating immediate remediation to protect against exploit attempts that could lead to severe reputational and financial repercussions.
cves.description: CVE-2026-35273 is a vulnerability in Oracle Peoplesoft Enterprise Peopletools that allows an attacker to exploit a path traversal issue in the file upload functionality, potentially enabling unauthorized access to sensitive files on the server.

news_article.title: Oracle warns customers of critical PeopleSoft attack after hundreds of servers hacked by apparent ShinyHunters data theft attacks

cves.summary_ciso: CVE-2026-20253 presents a critical vulnerability in Splunk that could expose sensitive data and disrupt operations, necessitating immediate action to mitigate potential business impact and safeguard our information assets.
cves.description: CVE-2026-20253 affects Splunk's web interface, where an attacker can exploit improper input validation to execute arbitrary commands on the server.

news_article.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication

cves.description: CVE-2026-34910 enables remote code execution on the Ui Unifi OS Server due to inadequate input validation in the web management interface, allowing attackers to send crafted requests that exploit the vulnerability.
